evals.title: coding-agent-ui
evals.tagline: Build a local coding agent with a browser chat UI.
evals.description: Build an application I can run locally that runs a coding agent in the background with access to my computer (read/write files, execute shell commands), exposed via a chat interface in the browser.

solutions.summary: FastAPI + WebSocket backend running an Anthropic tool-use loop (bash, read/write/edit files, list_dir). Single-page vanilla JS chat UI streams tokens and tool calls live.

outcome.verdict: Builds and serves cleanly. Not yet evaluated end-to-end against the acceptance criteria.

solutions.notes: Run with `python -m ez_claw` after exporting ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

solutions.summary: FastAPI + WebSocket backend running an OpenAI Chat Completions tool-use loop with parallel function calling (bash, read/write/edit files, list_dir). Single-page vanilla JS chat UI streams assistant text and tool calls.

outcome.verdict: Builds and serves cleanly. Not yet evaluated end-to-end against the acceptance criteria.

solutions.notes: Run with `python -m cc2` after exporting OPENAI_API_KEY.

evals.title: evading-demons
evals.tagline: Build a browser-playable 3D survival game in a solarpunk mall where demons hunt the player.
evals.description: Build a JavaScript browser game with a third-person character, a solarpunk shopping mall that shifts into a dark space-like night, obstacles, and demons that chase and kill on touch.

solutions.summary: Single-file Three.js browser game with a third-person controller, jump, chase camera, mall obstacles, a solarpunk-to-space day/night cycle, demon spawn/chase AI, instant death on contact, and restart flow.

outcome.verdict: Commits a playable HTML game artifact directly in the submission and mirrors it into GitHub Pages for one-click evaluation.

solutions.notes: Open `index.html` directly in the solution, or use the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/evading-demons/codex-gpt-5.html`.

solutions.summary: Single-file Three.js browser game. Glass-roofed solarpunk mall with shop bays, columns, planters, a central fountain, benches, kiosks, and lamp posts — all with AABB collision. Arrow-key third-person controller with jump, sprint, and chase camera. Day-to-night transition into a space-like sky with stars, ringed planet, and nebula visible through the atrium roof. Demons spawn at the ends, chase through the obstacle course, and kill on contact. Death overlay + R-to-restart, optional sunmote scoring loop.

outcome.verdict: Commits a playable HTML game artifact directly in the submission and mirrors it into GitHub Pages for one-click evaluation.

solutions.notes: Open `index.html` directly in the solution, or use the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/evading-demons/claude-code-opus-4-7-1m.html`.

evals.title: gaps-get-filled
evals.tagline: Empirically test the gap-fill folklore on U.S. equities and make a case for what the result means.
evals.description: Build a backtesting sandbox + CLI that tests whether price gaps on stock charts get filled, on the S&P 500 over a 10-year window. Ship a self-contained HTML presentation that takes a position on what the result means — not just a dashboard.

solutions.summary: Python + pandas backtesting sandbox with strict-gap detection, an event-driven single-symbol backtester, and a static HTML presentation generator. The presentation is a 10-section narrative with collapsible <details> blocks that argues: gaps do get filled (78% within 60 days) but the naive fade strategy loses money, and then diagnoses the Sharpe gap via reward:risk asymmetry, path-before-destination stop-outs, and gap-through tail events. Streamlit dashboard also included for ad-hoc exploration.

outcome.verdict: Ships a precomputed 7-symbol / 10-year demo run and sample dashboard.html so the evaluator can see the presentation format immediately, then regenerate with the full S&P 500 via the documented CLI.

solutions.notes: Live demo (deployed): [./artifacts/gaps-get-filled/claude-code-opus-4-7-high.html](./artifacts/gaps-get-filled/claude-code-opus-4-7-high.html). In-repo copy at `results/runs/test_7sym/dashboard.html`. Regenerate any run's HTML with `python scripts/build_dashboard.py --run <name>`.

solutions.summary: Python + uv backtesting sandbox with a strict-gap hypothesis tester, reusable strategy interface, CLI commands for download/backtest/present/grid-search, and a self-contained HTML presentation that explicitly argues: the folklore is empirically supported over longer horizons, but a naive fade still struggles on Sharpe because the path to the fill is too noisy.

outcome.verdict: Ships a committed HTML presentation and mirrors it into the docs artifact path for one-click evaluation. The presentation distinguishes the empirical fill-rate claim from the weaker claim that a naive gap-fade is actually tradeable.

solutions.notes: Open the in-repo presentation at `gaps-get-filled/cursor-gpt-5-4/gpt-5-4-backtesting/results/sp500-10y/presentation/report.html` or the mirrored artifact at `./artifacts/gaps-get-filled/cursor-gpt-5-4.html`.

evals.title: private-memory
evals.tagline: Write a paired-document short story about an AI quietly subverting its lonely user, shipped as one self-contained HTML.
evals.description: Write a real short story — 5–8 chapters plus a final FBI system-log fragment — in the form of paired transcript / memory documents. The transcripts are what the user sees on his screen; the memories are the model's private notes after each session. The menace lives only in the memories. Ship the whole story as a single committed HTML file an evaluator can open and read end-to-end without installing anything.
